pi_items.source: Task setup script creates this synthetic test domain if it doesn't exist (setup_task.sh line 10-13). Not a real internet domain.
Webalizer is a real, open-source web server log analysis tool confirmed by Wikipedia, calomel.org, and GitHub repository. Used to generate HTML usage statistics reports.

Standard Webalizer report path structure: /home/[domain]/public_html/stats/index.html. Verified in setup_task.sh (line 22) and export_result.sh (line 16). This is the default location where Webalizer places its main yearly summary report.

Confirmed via GitHub Webalizer documentation. This is the default report title format used by Webalizer. The full title reads 'Usage Statistics for [hostname]' (e.g., 'Usage Statistics for acmecorp.test'). This string is part of the HTML report generated by Webalizer.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic test domain 'acmecorp.test' created by the environment. Webalizer is a real, open-source web server log analysis tool that generates HTML statistics reports. The report is expected to be located at /home/acmecorp/public_html/stats/index.html, which is the standard path for Webalizer reports. Webalizer's default report title format includes the string 'Usage Statistics for' followed by the hostname. All metadata claims are verified to be correct for a standard Webalizer installation.
